tldr: Headlines across all eleven country-buckets lead overwhelmingly with the twin bomb blasts in Damascus during Macron's visit, while Syrian-based outlets also foreground the trip as a historic diplomatic and economic turning point for post-Assad Syria.

evidence.quote: explosions à Damas survenues pendant la visite de Macron
Bombs rock Damascus during Macron visit
The bombing that overshadowed Macron's historic Syria visit
Blasts hit Damascus during Macron visit
Damascus pivot: Macron's visit to Syria
visits Syria, 18 months after Assad's fall
First French Presidential Visit to Syria in 17 Years
Strategic Economic Renaissance at Damascus Roundtable
French President Vows to Help Rebuild Syria

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only outlet to frame the bombings as an intentional 'Secret Message' to Macron (scare-quoted, followed by a question mark), converting a reported security incident into a speculative, conspiratorial framing that no other headline in the corpus adopts — while simultaneously noting Macron's reconstruction pledge, producing the most tonally polarised headline in the set.

Only headline to ignore the bombings entirely and instead frame Macron's visit through the lens of its impact on French jihadists embedded in Syria, foregrounding a counter-terrorism/foreign-fighter angle invisible in every other outlet's title.

bottom_line: The headline layer frames Macron's Syria trip almost entirely as a security-incident story — bombs 'rocking' Damascus dominate across 10 of 11 buckets — with only Syrian-based press simultaneously advertising the visit's diplomatic milestone and economic reconstruction promise.
